key_insight: Trajectory-aware flow matching learns to generate feasible topology-optimised designs by using BESO optimisation history as a physics-guided transport path instead of relying on slow reverse diffusion or extra guidance at inference.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster design exploration for structural optimisation problems, producing multiple candidate layouts with better compliance, volume-fraction control, and topology fidelity.
method: Linear FMTO: interpolate between Gaussian noise and a BESO reference topology as the baseline transport path.
Trajectory-aware FMTO: build the probability path and target velocity field from volume-fraction-indexed BESO states.
Use trajectory weighting to inject optimisation history while keeping inference step counts low, with no extra optimisation at sampling time.
evidence_signal: Experiments show FMTO outperforms a diffusion-based baseline in sampling efficiency and design quality, and moderate trajectory weighting gives the best overall results especially under limited training data.
watch_item: Too much trajectory guidance can over-constrain the learned flow, so path design and weight selection need careful tuning to avoid reduced generative flexibility.
paper_title: Trajectory-Aware Flow Matching for Topology Optimisation
